verdict_scope: the GPU-BATCHED (jit/vmap) GF kernel was NOT delivered in this lane; the faithful CPU-NumPy reference REMAINS WRF-oracle-PASSING (see grellfreitas_savepoint_parity_report.json, unchanged).
honest_summary: A WRF-faithful jit/vmap GPU batching of Grell-Freitas was attempted-and-scoped but deliberately NOT shipped, because a faithful pure-JAX rewrite of the GF closure is a multi-thousand-LOC re-derivation that this single lane could not complete without high risk of silently breaking the (already-passing) WRF parity. Per the WRF-FAITHFUL-ONLY / no-fudge rule, GF is reported honestly as carried-over rather than shipped half-working. The CPU-reference GF gate (predeclared tol, real unmodified-WRF oracle, all 5 regimes PASS) is intact and authoritative.

cpu_reference_status.note: faithful single-column NumPy port; PASSES the unmodified-WRF GF module oracle across deep/shallow/non-triggering/scale-aware(coarse,fine) -- worst per-case rel residual 6.6e-3 vs 5e-2 tol.

oracle.source: unmodified WRF GF modules from /home/enric/src/wrf_pristine/WRF/phys compiled into a standalone single-column driver (module_cu_gf_deep.F, module_cu_gf_sh.F, module_cu_gf_wrfdrv.F + GFS support)
oracle.savepoints: proofs/v060/savepoints/gf_case_{1..5}.json (reused from the CPU-reference lane, bit-identical gold)

predeclared_gpu_batch_tolerance.status: NOT EXERCISED -- no GPU-batched kernel was produced to gate against this tolerance.
predeclared_gpu_batch_tolerance.note: the tolerance was predeclared (tighter than the 5e-2 CPU-reference gate) so that any future GF vmap kernel is gated honestly, not loosened to pass.

why_not_delivered.while_true_unbounded: cup_kbcon cap_inc search (module_cu_gf_deep.F: iterate cap-increment until KBCON found or KTF reached) -- a data-dependent, a-priori-unbounded iteration
why_not_delivered.data_dependent_gamma: get_zu_zd_pdf_fim computes math.gamma(alpha) where alpha is derived from per-level pressure ratios (data-dependent), for 4 draft types (UP/SH2/MID/DOWN)
why_not_delivered.closure_ensemble: cup_forcing_ens_3d: 16-member dynamic-control closure ensemble (MAXENS3) with diurnal cycle + per-member control flow
why_not_delivered.iterative_downdraft: cup_dd_moisture / cup_dd_edt iterative downdraft with ierr short-circuiting
why_not_delivered.ierr_shortcircuit: pervasive ierr-based block-skipping (cup_env/cup_kbcon/etc. return early when ierr!=0) -- every block must be re-expressed as jnp.where masking on a traced ierr
why_not_delivered.assessment: Each obstacle is individually convertible (math.gamma -> jax.scipy.special.gamma; break-searches -> jnp.argmax over masks; while-true -> bounded fori_loop + mask; ierr -> jnp.where), but the aggregate is a ~1500-2000 LOC faithful re-derivation across ~21 subroutines. Doing it safely (without silently breaking the passing WRF parity) is a dedicated sprint, not a lane sub-task. The prior Opus GF handoff (.agent/reviews/2026-06-03-opus-v060-gf2.md) independently reached the same conclusion: 'a batched/JAX vectorization is a separate optimization sprint (GF is NOT load-bearing for v1.0.0 -- KF done, Tiedtke covers the menu).'

scan_wiring_status: cu_physics=3 remains FAIL-CLOSED in the operational scan (_resolve_operational_suite rejects it loudly with the documented reason). It is ACCEPTED in physics_registry + namelist_check (frozen S0 contract) but NOT scan-wired -- this is the honest, fail-closed state, never a silent no-op.
recommended_next_step: Dedicated GF-vmap sprint: faithfully translate the ~21 subroutines above to lax-traceable JAX, gate against these same gf_case_{1..5} savepoints at the predeclared 2e-2 tolerance. NOT a v1.0.0 blocker: KF(1) + Tiedtke(6) already cover the operational cumulus menu.
